name: Iznájar Reservoir Loop
name_original: Hasta el Embalse de Iznájar — circular por el norte de la Vega de Granada
description: Tackle this difficult 46.5-mile road cycling route from the Vega de Granada to the Embalse de Iznájar, featuring diverse landscapes.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Loja, Spain, traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills, extensive olive groves, and mountainous terrain. The region features varied elevations, including significant climbs and descents, offering diverse cycling experiences. Routes often pass by reservoirs and through agricultural areas, providing scenic views and engaging rides. Best road cycling routes around Loja The most popular road cycling route is Source of the Hundred Spouts and Archidona — circular from Loja, a 48.2 miles (77.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 57 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation changes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Iznájar Reservoir Loop, a difficult 46.5 miles (74.8 km) path. This route includes substantial climbs and descents around the Iznájar Reservoir. Local road cyclists also love the Mount Hacho Loop from Loja, a 28.8 miles (46.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 14 minutes. Road cycling around Loja is defined by rolling hills, extensive olive groves, and mountainous terrain. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Loja are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from 3 reviews. More than 500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Loja's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Loja?
A: There are over 35 road cycling routes around Loja, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. You'll find a good mix of moderate and difficult trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling near Loja?
A: Road cycling routes around Loja traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills, extensive olive groves, and mountainous terrain. You can expect varied elevations, including significant climbs and descents, providing engaging rides through scenic agricultural areas and past reservoirs.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or less experienced road cyclists?
A: While many routes in Loja are considered difficult, there are 11 moderate routes available that might be more suitable for less experienced road cyclists looking for a challenge without extreme difficulty. These routes still feature varied terrain and elevation changes.

Q: What are the most challenging road cycling routes in the Loja area?
A: For experienced road cyclists seeking a challenge, Loja offers 24 difficult routes. Notable options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983220613/source-of-the-hundred-spouts-and-archidona-circular-from-loja" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Hundred Spouts and Archidona — circular from Loja&lt;/a&gt;, a 77.6 km trail with significant elevation changes, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983229565/iznajar-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iznájar Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 74.8 km path featuring substantial climbs and descents.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Loja?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Loja are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983220613/source-of-the-hundred-spouts-and-archidona-circular-from-loja" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Hundred Spouts and Archidona — circular from Loja&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983379754/mount-hacho-loop-from-loja" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mount Hacho Loop from Loja&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Loja?
A: The road cycling routes in Loja are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Over 500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the scenic views and engaging rides through olive groves and mountainous landscapes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in Loja?
A: Given the prevalence of rolling hills and significant elevation changes, most road cycling routes around Loja are categorized as moderate to difficult. While there are no routes specifically labeled 'easy,' families with older children or those with good fitness levels might enjoy some of the moderate options, though caution is advised due to traffic on roads.

Q: Can I bring my dog on road cycling routes around Loja?
A: Road cycling routes typically follow paved roads and are not generally suitable for dogs, especially given the distances and potential traffic. For activities with dogs, you might consider exploring walking or hiking trails in the region instead.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Loja?
A: The best time for road cycling in Loja is generally during the spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder and more comfortable for sustained activity. Summers can be quite hot, and winters may bring cooler temperatures and occasional rain.

Q: Are there places to park my car near the road cycling routes in Loja?
A: Parking availability can vary depending on the specific starting point of your chosen route. In Loja itself, you can often find street parking or public car parks. For routes starting outside the city, look for designated parking areas in smaller towns or villages, or near popular attractions.

Q: How can I reach the road cycling routes around Loja using public transport?
A: Loja is well-connected by bus services within the region, which can help you reach the city center. However, accessing specific rural starting points for road cycling routes via public transport can be challenging. It's often more convenient to drive or arrange local transport to reach the exact starting location of your chosen route.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer views of the extensive olive groves and mountainous terrain characteristic of the region. You can also encounter natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/388446" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hundred‑Spout Fountain&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5061410" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baños de Vilo Hot Springs&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983229565/iznajar-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iznájar Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, provide scenic views of the reservoir itself.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the road cycling routes in Loja?
A: Yes, given the mountainous terrain, many routes offer scenic viewpoints. For instance, you might find panoramic views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4859686" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gomer and Doña Ana cliffs&lt;/a&gt;. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31450633" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montefrío – View of Olive Groves loop from Loja&lt;/a&gt; are specifically designed to showcase the region's beautiful landscapes.
tips: Zagra is a town surrounded by hills planted with olive trees. The town is dominated by an old castle that stands on a rock, elevated from the rest of the buildings.

Iznájar is located on the banks of the Genil river, which forms the Iznájar swamp as it passes through the municipality. Its location, on a large stone, means that it represents a famous nucleus. Also through the narrow streets and white houses in the middle of a small cape surrounded by water.
From the road that passes on the other side of the Iznájar reservoir you can see the views of the town itself, which is located on the marsh offering a special contrast!

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of this racebike route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as difficult. It features significant elevation gains, with around 1700 meters of climbing, and covers a distance of nearly 75 kilometers. Cyclists should expect varied terrain, including sections through the agricultural plains of the Vega de Granada and more challenging, rolling hills around the Embalse de Iznájar.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this ride?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite diverse. You'll experience relatively flat or gently undulating sections through the fertile agricultural areas of the Vega de Granada, likely passing olive and orange groves. As you approach the Embalse de Iznájar, the route transitions to more rolling hills and some significant climbs, characteristic of the region's 'slightly sloped hills' and 'rugged mountain roads'.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: This circular route offers several notable features. You'll pass by the historic Zagra Castle and ride through the charming Iznájar Village, perched on a hill overlooking the reservoir. Expect stunning Views of Iznájar and the expansive Embalse de Iznájar itself, often called the 'lake of Andalusia'.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this racebike tour?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for this exact starting point aren't provided, for a circular route of this length, it's generally advisable to find parking in one of the larger towns or villages along the Vega de Granada or near Iznájar. Look for public parking areas that can accommodate vehicles for several hours.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating, significant distance (nearly 75 km), and substantial elevation gain (around 1700 meters), this racebike route is not recommended for beginners. It's a challenging ride that requires good fitness and experience with varied terrain and climbs.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region generally offers good cycling conditions outside of the peak summer months. Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) are often ideal, providing pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be very hot, making the significant climbs particularly demanding, while winters might bring cooler temperatures and a higher chance of rain.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this racebike route?
faqs.answer: This is a racebike route primarily on roads. While there are no specific restrictions against dogs on public roads, it's generally not suitable for cycling with a dog due to the speed, distance, and traffic considerations. If you plan to stop in villages, always check local regulations regarding pets.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, this racebike route primarily uses public roads and trails, so you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle it.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, while primarily a road cycling route, it does briefly intersect with several local paths. You might encounter very short sections that cross or run alongside paths such as the Camino del Cortijo del Chopo, Cordel de Loja a Iznájar, Sendero Las Chorreras, and Camino Bajo, among others. These are generally very minor overlaps.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or drink along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through or near several villages, most notably Iznájar Village. These settlements typically offer cafes, bars, and restaurants where you can refuel and take a break. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for the longer, more remote sections.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging racebike tour?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, you should bring plenty of water, energy gels or bars, and essential bike repair tools (spare tubes, pump, multi-tool). Appropriate cycling attire, including a helmet, gloves, and sunglasses, is crucial. Sunscreen is also highly recommended, especially during warmer months. A fully charged phone and a GPS device with the route loaded are also advisable.
